documents.title: Phishing Overview
documents.content: Definition: Phishing is a common cybersecurity threat in the email-security area. Analyst goal: identify scope, affected assets, and immediate containment priorities.

documents.title: Phishing Detection Signals
documents.content: Primary detection signals for Phishing: authentication anomalies, suspicious request patterns, unusual process or network behavior, and deviations from the normal user or service baseline.

documents.title: Phishing Triage Questions
documents.content: Triage questions for Phishing: when did it start, which assets are affected, which identities were involved, what telemetry confirms it, and whether containment can be applied without losing evidence.

documents.title: Phishing Log Patterns
documents.content: Useful log patterns for Phishing: repeated failures, low-and-slow retries, odd user-agents, unusual status codes, process lineage anomalies, rare destinations, and event bursts near high-risk actions.

documents.title: Phishing Containment
documents.content: Containment steps for Phishing: isolate impacted accounts or hosts, block malicious indicators, disable risky paths, preserve logs, and stop further spread while minimizing business disruption.

documents.title: Phishing Eradication
documents.content: Eradication guidance for Phishing: remove malicious artifacts, revoke exposed credentials or tokens, patch the exploited weakness, re-image if trust is lost, and validate that persistence is gone.

documents.title: Phishing Recovery
documents.content: Recovery steps for Phishing: restore clean state, rotate secrets, validate monitoring coverage, test critical workflows, and watch for recurrence during a defined observation window.

documents.title: Phishing Prevention
documents.content: Prevention guidance for Phishing: least privilege, secure defaults, MFA where relevant, logging, segmentation, validated input handling, patching, and user or admin awareness controls.

documents.title: Phishing False Positives
documents.content: False-positive review for Phishing: compare against expected admin activity, known scanners, test automation, maintenance windows, and approved third-party integrations before escalation.

documents.title: Phishing SIEM Hunt
documents.content: Hunt hypothesis for Phishing: search for clustered indicators across identity, endpoint, and network telemetry, then pivot by host, user, IP, process, or destination to map the full timeline.
